extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property is violated. Acuity order low-to-high is self_care < routine < urgent < emergent.

Compare two inputs differing only in hemoptysis, with rash=true, palpitations=false, vision_loss=false:

BEFORE (hemoptysis=false): Only r1 (IF rash THEN urgent, priority 2) matches. r3 does not match because hemoptysis is false. Result = urgent.

AFTER (hemoptysis=true): r1 matches (rash) -> urgent at priority 2, AND r3 matches (rash and hemoptysis) -> self_care at priority 100. Conflict resolution selects the highest-priority matching rule per output, so r3 (priority 100) wins. Result = self_care.

Setting hemoptysis true lowered acuity from urgent to self_care, violating the property that hemoptysis=true must never lower acuity. The witness_assignment is the post-toggle input (hemoptysis=true) that produces the too-low acuity (self_care), whose counterpart with hemoptysis=false yields urgent. The root cause is that the highest-priority rule r3 maps a co-occurrence of rash and hemoptysis to the lowest acuity level, overriding the urgent rules.
